dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.reminder: Reminder active — go to phone Settings to silence alerts manually ({{count}} schedule(s))

setup.title: Enable Silent Robot
setup.info: The robot needs Accessibility Service permission to navigate your phone Settings automatically.
setup.step1Desc: Tap the button below to open your phone's Accessibility settings.
setup.step2Desc: Scroll to find "Silent Robot" under Installed Services or Downloaded Apps.
setup.step3Desc: Tap Silent Robot, then tap "Use Service" and confirm.
setup.waitingHint: Waiting for you to enable the service…
setup.enabledDesc: The accessibility service is active. You can now use Quick Actions or record custom sequences.

quickActions.title: Quick Actions
quickActions.desc: Run a built-in sequence to toggle Wireless Emergency Alerts or Airplane Mode on your device.

record.title: Record New Sequence
record.idleHint: Record a custom UI sequence by tapping through your phone's settings. Each tap is captured.

list.title: Saved Recordings
list.empty: No recordings yet. Use Quick Actions above or record a custom sequence.

webNotice.title: Android App Required
webNotice.desc: The Robot feature requires the Silent Android app with Accessibility Service. Download the APK and install it on your Android device.
webNotice.step1: Download the APK using the button above
webNotice.step2: On your Android device, go to Settings → Security → enable "Install from Unknown Sources"
webNotice.step3: Open the downloaded APK file and tap Install
webNotice.step4: Open Silent and go to Robot → follow the setup guide

scheduler.title: Scheduler
scheduler.noSchedules: No schedules yet. Create one to get started.
scheduler.browserNote: ⚠️ This app cannot silence alerts automatically — WEA messages bypass all software. Schedules remind you when to go to your device Settings and toggle off alerts manually.
scheduler.name: Schedule Name

logs.title: Logs
logs.noLogs: No logs to show. Actions will be logged here.

about.title: About
about.bio1: With over 4 years of experience, I've built 50+ projects ranging from web applications to automation systems and AI-powered tools.
about.bio2: Passionate about writing clean, maintainable code and staying at the forefront of web development. Always learning, always building.
about.appDesc: Silent is an open-source emergency alert scheduler that helps you manage when your device silences government emergency notifications.
about.disclaimerText: Emergency alerts exist to save lives. Silencing them means you may not receive critical safety information in real time. Use this tool responsibly.
about.exp1b1: Developed 50+ production applications and tools using React, Node.js, and TypeScript
about.exp1b2: Integrated AI/ML models including Claude API, Gemini, and OpenAI into applications
about.exp1b3: Built automation workflows using N8N and GitHub Actions for enterprise clients
about.exp1b4: Implemented real-time features using WebSockets and modern React patterns
about.exp1b5: Deployed applications on Vercel, Render, and GCP with CI/CD pipelines
about.exp2b1: Contributed to multiple open-source projects with focus on developer tools and utilities
about.exp2b2: Maintained repositories with comprehensive documentation and CI/CD pipelines
about.exp2b3: Collaborated with community members on bug fixes and feature implementations
about.exp2b4: Created educational content and tutorials for developers

help.title: Help
help.contactDesc: Have a question, bug report, or feature request? Send a message directly.

faq.q1: How do I silence emergency alerts on iPhone?
faq.a1: Go to Settings → Notifications → scroll to bottom → Government Alerts. Turn off "Extreme Alert" and "Public Safety Alerts".
faq.q2: How do I silence emergency alerts on Android?
faq.a2: Go to Settings → Safety & Emergency → Wireless Emergency Alerts → toggle off "Allow alerts" or specific alert types.
faq.a3: A schedule marks a time window during which the app reminds you to silence your alerts. It does not automatically change your phone settings — you must manually toggle them.
faq.a4: "none" = one-time window, "daily" = every day, "weekly" = specific days of week, "custom" = date-range with any settings.
faq.a5: The theme smoothly transitions from light (at noon) to dark (at midnight) using a cosine curve. It updates every 5 seconds.
faq.a6: All schedules, logs, and settings are stored locally in your browser's localStorage. Nothing is sent to any server.
faq.q7: How do I contact the developer?
faq.a7: Use the contact form below to send a message directly. Fill in your name, email, subject, and message, then click Send.

form.name: Your Name
form.subject: Subject
form.success: Message sent! I'll get back to you soon.
form.captchaNote: This site is protected by reCAPTCHA and the Google Privacy Policy and Terms of Service apply.
